quote_of_day.text:
>>49322
be honest to yourself and look through all events you have seen throughout your life, lafde pange of people around you, think of school times and how nanhejeets fought each other, and then say again that it is not real. please think a bit before reacting.
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of caste hatred moved into the board, bringing with it a squall of Dravidian-Aryan rift discussions. The daily thread on the topic., with Anon debating the association of Aryans with India and Indians.

A scattered drizzle of subscription service discussions fell across the board, as Anon sought opinions on streaming services in India, specifically Netflix's 200rs monthly price. The conversation.

Meanwhile, a brief but intense downpour of gossip swept through the board, as Anon shared rumors of secret hookups between senior boys from Doon and Mayo. The discussion was marked by 54 posts.

Elsewhere, a cold snap of hostility chilled the board, as Anon suggested targeting groups for rape, sparking 20 posts. A separate thread on dealing with streetshitters and suggesting better names.

articles.title: Dravidian-Aryan Rift Spills onto Indiachan
articles.body:
Anon kicked off a discussion on Indiachan's /b/ board, suggesting that "Aryans should stop associating themselves with India and Indians (Dravidians)." One respondent claimed that the ancient Indian empire was established by "NW warriors" from the region of Takshshila, and that Dravidians had ties to the area.

Another anon pushed back, saying ">these pajeets were ruled by biharis for centuries" was "irrelevant historical info" and that the point was moot.

The conversation turned to the topic of creating a separate online space for Dravidians, with one anon suggesting that it would inevitably lead to "balkanisation" and the involvement of "pajeet feds." A different anon floated the idea that North Indians, or "Northies," should need to create an account to post on a site, while South Indians, or "Southies," could post without one.

This, they claimed, was to prevent spammers. The thread also touched on genetics and physical characteristics, with one anon stating that Dravidians shared genetic similarities with people from Bihar.

The thread revealed a complex web of identity politics and historical grievances, with some anons advocating for separation and others pushing back against what they saw as attempts to "kang" or co-opt their cultural identity.

Anon janny "buckbroke abbos" called for the removal of a user, while another suggested that the site's current setup was preferable to creating a new, Dravidian-only space.

The discussion highlighted the ongoing tensions between different groups on Indiachan, with no clear resolution in sight.

articles.title: Streaming subscriptions and piracy in India
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on streaming subscriptions in India, asking others about their experiences and opinions on various services.

They compared prices of Netflix and Jio Hotstar, noting that Netflix costs 200 rupees a month for one device, while Jio Hotstar can be had for 100 rupees a month with a 350 rupees for three months plan for two devices.

Another anon responded, saying they didn't need to own a TV to consume content and that they had a TV only because their parents bought it. They found the prices to be reasonable.

A different anon mentioned that they pirate content because they dislike the idea of accumulating multiple subscriptions for limited content.

One anon shared that they used to pirate content but stopped after listening to Arpit Bala's argument that piracy hurts the creation of games and content in India.

However, others disagreed, arguing that if everyone pirated, it would harm the creators. Anon countered that corporations still pay creators, even if they don't care much about customers. Some anons discussed their own experiences with subscriptions.

One college student mentioned that they save up 100 rupees a month and buy a three-month Jio Hotstar subscription.

Another anon pays for Crunchyroll, which they regret, and also gets Jio Hotstar and Amazon Prime with their Jio and WiFi.

The discussion also touched on the topic of piracy, with one anon boasting of pirating the World Cup in better quality than Zee5. Others mentioned that they only pay for content when it's not available through other means.

One anon suggested that a private theater experience at a mall would be a better option than multiple subscriptions.

Anons also discussed their income and spending habits, with one delivery agent mentioning that they work only on certain days to earn some extra money.

The conversation revealed a range of perspectives on streaming subscriptions, piracy, and content consumption in India.

articles.title: Doon and Mayo Senior Boys' Secret Hookups Exposed
articles.body:
Anon started a thread claiming that senior boys from Doon and Mayo schools were caught hooking up after unofficial basketball games. These gatherings involved booking venues for weekends and staying overnight. The school administration is reportedly trying to contain the situation.

Another anon chimed in, saying this kind of behavior isn't surprising and happens every year. They claimed the administration is aware of it, but it's only a problem if it leaks out or bothers parents.

One respondent noted that in their experience, wealth didn't play a significant role in how they were treated at school.

Anon shared that they graduated from Doon three years ago and recalled the parties fondly, especially the ones where they'd book villas for winter breaks.

These events involved both guys and girls, and they described them as 'to literally die for.' A different anon expressed surprise that this behavior is considered scandalous, given the school's reputation.

The conversation turned to the topic of wealth and social dynamics at Doon. One anon mentioned that their family's income wasn't openly discussed, and they didn't know their family's exact financial situation.

Another respondent claimed that in their experience, there wasn't a significant class system based on wealth. Anon joked about the school's demographics, saying it's either full of short, less intelligent kids or tall, beautiful guys.

A respondent shared that they met their boyfriend at Doon, and another anon noted that while they were busy preparing for exams, they did date during their time there.

The discussion also touched on similar behaviors in other schools, with one anon mentioning certain Swiss schools where similar incidents occur. The conversation remained focused on the behaviors and dynamics within elite schools, with various anons sharing their experiences and perspectives.

articles.title: Anon suggests new name, deals with streetshitters
articles.body: Anon opened a thread suggesting a better name for Indiachan and ways to deal with streetshitters. Another anon replied that they didn't want demographics on the site, which they believed was heavily Bimarud, and suggested the name 'Induschan'. Anon also discussed Aryan and Dravidian admixture, with one claiming all Indians have Aryan admixture. A respondent asked if they, as a Himachali Brahmin, were considered Aryan.

articles.title: Anon suggests targeting groups for rape
articles.body:
Anon started the thread saying, 'Can we all just rape kanglus and dalits instead of north south?' Anon responded, 'South Indians are mostly Dalits so..'.

Another Anon said, 'Caste baiters are mostly shoodra-chamars.' One Anon claimed, 'Nah 80% of us are shudras, dalits were bought here by aryans like sage agastya'. Anon also stated, 'It's always been the muzzies larping and creating division.

Such repulsive creatures they are.' Anon remarked, 'Do you really think all thise abbo subbo niggers are innocent'.
